title: Fleet: re-read policy fleet block (count/min_lanes) at every wave boundary instead of batch-start snapshot
summary: Live incident 2026-07-12 (~20:17): operator committed fleet.min_lanes 3->10 mid-batch (sanctioned ship a33ffd6a) to hold width 10 under a codex quota bench, but wave 1 still dispatched 'wave count 10 -> 9 (min 3)' — the supervisor snapshots the fleet policy block at batch start and never re-reads it, so a committed control-plane change requires a full supervisor bounce (kills in-flight lanes) to take effect. This is asymmetric: the control-plane preflight already re-validates policy.json CLEANLINESS per wave (it refuses dirty control-plane files at every dispatch), yet dispatch consumes stale VALUES. Fix the class: at each wave boundary, before QuotaAwareCount, re-resolve the fleet block (count, min_lanes, plan_source) from the committed policy.json — single resolution point, same loader as batch start; log '[loop] fleet config reloaded: count=N min_lanes=M' when values changed. Operator width directives (user standing rule: 10 lanes ALL THE TIME) must take effect at the next wave without killing lanes.
acceptance: RED: TestFleetDispatch_ReloadsMinLanesAtWaveBoundary (commit min_lanes change between waves; wave N+1 uses new floor)
RED: TestFleetDispatch_UnchangedPolicyByteIdenticalDispatch (regression pin: no policy change => identical wave config)
